name: open-forge
description: Self-host open-source apps on your own cloud, your own VPS, your own Kubernetes cluster, a PaaS, or your own laptop. Chat-friendly interface to existing tools (aws, az, hcloud, doctl, gcloud, flyctl, kubectl, helm, podman, docker, lume, tailscale, ssh, gh) — Claude runs every command; you only answer what/where/how and provide credentials. Supported software: Ghost, OpenClaw, Hermes-Agent, Ollama, Open WebUI, Stable Diffusion WebUI (Automatic1111), ComfyUI. AI stack focus: Ollama (LLM inference) + Open WebUI (multi-user UI) + Stable Diffusion WebUI / ComfyUI (image generation — A1111 for tabbed-form UX, ComfyUI for node-based workflows) compose into a complete local-AI stack; OpenClaw + Hermes-Agent are agent projects that point at Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible backend. A1111 and ComfyUI both plug into Open WebUI as image-gen backends and share models via `extra_model_paths.yaml`. Every recipe covers upstream-blessed install methods (or the most-active community Docker fork where upstream ships no first-party container, as with A1111 and ComfyUI).
